title: Bruins’ prospect James Hagens turns pro, will begin career in Providence
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens signed an AHL amateur tryout with Providence Bruins, leaving Boston College after a standout sophomore year.
Hagens is the Bruins' 2025 seventh overall draft pick, known for high scoring and compared to NHL star Jack Hughes.
He'll practice with Providence on Tuesday and is expected to play against the Springfield Thunderbirds on Wednesday.

title: 7 X-factors on 2026 Red Sox roster
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Roman Anthony is viewed as a potential MVP candidate, expected to lead off for the Red Sox in 2026.
Brayan Bello, part of Boston's pitching rotation, aims to build on last year's progress and strengthen the team's starting lineup.
Marcelo Mayer's performance and durability are crucial, as he transitions to an infield role for the Red Sox this season.

title: State House Minority Leader opts not to run again after 32 years in office
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: State Rep. Bradley Jones announced he will not seek reelection after 32 years in office.
Jones, 61, will continue serving the 20th Middlesex District until January 2027.
Jones has been the Massachusetts House Minority Leader since 2002, elected 17 times since 1994.

title: No ICE agents at Logan Airport yet as agents deploy to busy airports
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: ICE agents have not been deployed to Logan Airport or any New England airports despite President Trump's federal orders.
Massport confirmed no ICE presence at Logan, while DHS cited operational security for not disclosing deployments in New England.
ICE agents have been sent to 14 airports facing long wait times, avoiding Logan, which remains operational without major delays.

title: Fitchburg fire chief on paid leave pending external investigation, mayor says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Fitchburg Fire Chief Dante Suarez is on paid leave as an external investigation is conducted, according to Mayor Sam Squailia.
Assistant Chief Patrick Haverty is acting as the fire department's chief during Suarez's absence, the mayor announced.
Suarez has been on leave since February 26, 2026, with no further details disclosed due to personnel privacy concerns.

title: Person seriously injured in Seaport stabbing
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A man was seriously injured after being stabbed in the abdomen and leg in Seaport on Sunday night.
Boston Police found the victim at 2:44 a.m. near Kings Dining and Entertainment on Seaport Blvd.
The victim was transported to a local hospital; no suspects have been identified, and the investigation is ongoing.

title: Here’s what happened during the first day of Kelsey Fitzsimmons’s trial
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons's trial began in Essex County Superior Court; she waived her right to a jury trial.
Prosecutors allege Fitzsimmons aimed a gun at Officer Patrick Noonan, leading to her being shot and hospitalized.
Defense argues Fitzsimmons intended to harm herself, not Noonan, and claims the shooting was misrepresented as self-defense.

title: Bruins host Blackstone Valley co-op hockey team at practice after run to R.I. state title ​
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins hosted the Blackstone Valley co-op hockey team at Warrior Ice Arena after their Rhode Island state championship win.
Blackstone Valley captain Colin Dorgan scored crucial goals following a personal tragedy, leading to their first state title victory.
Bruins players, including Jeremy Swayman, interacted with Blackstone Valley, celebrating the local team's achievements and resilience.

title: Patriots sign offensive tackle James Hudson
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Patriots signed offensive tackle James Hudson, who previously played for the Giants and Browns in his NFL career.
Hudson has played 60 games with 19 starts, primarily at right tackle but also has experience at left tackle.
He will add depth to the Patriots' offensive line, backing up current starters Will Campbell and Morgan Moses.

title: As waiting game continues with James Hagens, Bruins coach has no issue adding prospects to lineup
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens, a top Bruins prospect, has not yet signed a professional contract after finishing his sophomore season at Boston College.
Hagens scored 47 points in 34 games this season, helping Boston College win its first Beanpot title since 2016.
Bruins' coach Marco Sturm is open to integrating prospects into the lineup if they are ready to transition to professional play.
